source: Extracted from mif-ingest-to-lakehouse-infra-dev repo via Copilot analysis (cell1-cell8) + mentor SOP doc

topic_naming.description: Kafka topics are always environment-prefixed and follow a strict 4-segment dot-notation format

segment_map.segment_3: data_state (must always be 'raw' for raw-layer ingestion)

topic_naming.failure_message: Topic name does not match required pattern: {env}.{source_system}.{schema_grain}.raw

topic_naming.description: All raw-layer Kafka topics must end with the literal segment 'raw'
topic_naming.failure_message: Topic must end with '.raw'. Example: dev.saptcc.multi-1.raw

topic_naming.rule_name: Topic segment count must be exactly 4
topic_naming.description: Topics must have exactly 4 dot-separated segments
topic_naming.failure_message: Topic must have exactly 4 segments separated by dots: {env}.{source}.{grain}.raw

topic_naming.rule_name: Environment must be one of allowed values
topic_naming.description: First segment must be a recognized environment identifier
topic_naming.failure_message: Environment segment must be one of: dev, snd, prod

topic_naming.rule_name: Source system must be lowercase alphanumeric with optional hyphens
topic_naming.description: Source system segment should be lowercase, alphanumeric, may include hyphens
topic_naming.failure_message: Source system must be lowercase alphanumeric (hyphens allowed, no underscores or spaces)

source_system.rule_name: One top-level folder per source system
source_system.description: Each source system maps to exactly one top-level repository folder. Folder name = source system identity.
Before creating a new folder, check if source_system folder already exists in the repository
source_system.action_if_exists: Add new job entry to existing locals.tf in that folder
source_system.action_if_not_exists: Create new folder with source_system name, then create locals.tf and glue.tf matching repo patterns

source_system.rule_name: New source system requires vela.py registration
source_system.description: Every new top-level folder must be added to .vela.py for CI/CD deployment
source_system.failure_message: New source folder must be registered in .vela.py before deployment

folder_creation.rule_name: New folder must be registered in .vela.py
folder_creation.description: All deployable source folders must be listed in .vela.py

folder_creation.rule_name: New folder must use recognized deployment pattern
folder_creation.description: New folders should use one of the two primary Terraform patterns
allowed_patterns: locals.tf + glue.tf (local module pattern) — recommended for Kafka-to-Iceberg

folder_creation.rule_name: Folder name must match source system identity
folder_creation.description: Folder name is used as source identity in CI, secrets, databases, and warehouse paths

job_naming.description: Kafka-to-Iceberg batch jobs must follow the standard naming pattern

job_naming.rule_name: Job name must be unique within folder's glue_jobs map
job_naming.description: No two jobs in the same locals.tf glue_jobs map can have the same key
job_naming.failure_message: Job name already exists in this source folder's locals.tf

job_naming.description: Job names must use lowercase alphanumeric characters and hyphens only

worker_config.rule_name: Worker type must be from allowed set
worker_config.description: Only AWS Glue supported worker types are allowed
worker_config.failure_message: Worker type must be one of: G.025X, G.1X, G.2X, G.4X

worker_config.rule_name: Number of workers must be within allowed range
worker_config.description: Worker count must be between 1 and 10 inclusive
worker_config.failure_message: Number of workers must be between 1 and 10

worker_config.description: Convention-based sizing recommendations by workload class

enterprise_func.rule_name: Enterprise function must be from allowed set
enterprise_func.description: ent_func controls ownership tagging and CI-injected locals. Must be a recognized business domain.
enterprise_func.failure_message: Enterprise function must be one of: AGTR, CORP, FOOD, SPEC

enterprise_func.description: If the requested ent_func is not in the known list, treat as a governance-level change
enterprise_func.failure_message: Unknown ent_func — requires governance review before use

subgroup.description: Every source folder must declare a subgroup value
subgroup.note: Subgroup is less normalized than ent_func. New values are allowed but should follow existing patterns.

job_type.rule_name: Terraform job_type must be from allowed set
job_type.description: The Terraform job_type field controls Talaria execution mode
job_type.failure_message: job_type must be one of: unified, unified_batch, kafka_to_iceberg, kafka_to_iceberg_batch, flat_file_to_iceberg

job_type.description: Separate from job_type, the scheduling mode controls when the job runs
job_type.note: If 'scheduled', a trigger_schedule (cron expression) is required

job_type.rule_name: Cron expression format for scheduled jobs
job_type.description: AWS EventBridge cron format required for scheduled jobs
job_type.format: cron(minute hour day month day-of-week year)
job_type.failure_message: Cron expression must follow AWS EventBridge format: cron(0 1 * * ? *)

job_version.description: Controls which Talaria script/library artifacts are used
job_version.note: Use default unless specific version is required. Check with team for latest stable version.

sink_database.rule_name: Target database naming family A — minerva pattern
sink_database.description: Primary naming pattern for minerva-owned databases

sink_database.rule_name: Target database naming family B — lh pattern
sink_database.description: Alternative naming pattern for lh-prefixed databases
Environment must be encoded in the database name

sink_database.rule_name: Database name must include raw state
sink_database.description: Raw state must be indicated in the database name

s3_warehouse.description: S3 warehouse path prefix must always end with / for consistent prefix handling
s3_warehouse.failure_message: S3 warehouse path must end with /

s3_warehouse.rule_name: Warehouse path must include source system identity
s3_warehouse.description: The final path segment must include the source system name

s3_checkpoint.description: Iceberg streaming checkpoints must use the standard checkpoint bucket

secret_naming.description: AWS Secrets Manager secret for Kafka authentication credentials
secret_naming.note: Same secret is often reused for both source Kafka access and sink_transformer1 (kafka_split)

schema_registry.rule_name: Schema registry endpoint must be environment-mapped
schema_registry.description: Schema registry endpoints are defined as environment maps in locals.tf

iceberg_config.rule_name: Iceberg catalog type must be glue
iceberg_config.description: All Iceberg sinks in this repository use AWS Glue as the catalog

iceberg_config.rule_name: Iceberg catalog ID must be environment-mapped
iceberg_config.description: Glue catalog account ID differs per environment
Standard session name for cross-account Iceberg writes

transformer_chain.description: Standard transformer sequence for all Kafka-to-Iceberg raw jobs
transformer_chain.note: Use default chain unless customization is specifically requested

locals_tf_update.rule_name: locals.tf is the primary business configuration layer
locals_tf_update.description: All job definitions, environment maps, and ownership metadata live in locals.tf

locals_tf_update.rule_name: For existing folder: only update locals.tf
locals_tf_update.description: If the folder already has glue.tf wired up, only add a new entry to the glue_jobs map in locals.tf

locals_tf_update.rule_name: For new folder: create both locals.tf and glue.tf
locals_tf_update.description: New source folders require both files to be created matching existing repo patterns

glue_tf_update.description: glue.tf iterates over local.glue_jobs using for_each, it should NOT hold business config

glue_tf_update.rule_name: Update glue.tf only when creating a new folder
glue_tf_update.description: When adding jobs to an existing folder, do NOT modify glue.tf — only modify locals.tf

sink_trigger.description: For Kafka-to-Iceberg streaming jobs the sink trigger should be availableNow
